programs.title: Breathing new life into century-old Yueju Opera
programs.desc: Yueju Opera is one of the five major Chinese operas. It is skillful in narration and sentiment and is popular across China. This timeless form of intangible cultural heritage now has new apprentices. We followed a young duo of Yueju Opera performers: eleven-year-old Wang Yuhan and her stage partner, nine-year-old Huang Xinru in Ningbo, Zhejiang Province, as they shared kindred spirits in inheriting the performing art of Yueju Opera.

programs.title: Wudang Martial Arts and Taichi
programs.desc: Join host Yegor on a journey into the heart of China’s Wudang Mountains—the spiritual cradle of Taoism and the birthplace of Wudang martial arts. Through in-depth conversations with renowned masters such as Zhong Xueyong, Cai Chongguang, Jake Pinnick, and Liu Xiang, this episode explores the philosophy, techniques, and living spirit of Wudang Quan and Taichi.\nFrom the internal cultivation of qi to the harmony between body and mind, Wudang martial arts are more than just physical training—they are a way of life rooted in the Tao. Alongside martial techniques, the program also delves into traditional Chinese music, calligraphy, and medicine, revealing how these disciplines come together in the pursuit of balance, self-awareness, and longevity.\nThis is not just a story of punches and postures, but of tradition, transformation, and the timeless search for harmony between humans and the universe.

programs.title: Suzhou Fans
programs.desc: Suzhou, known as the “Venice of China,” is also famous for its traditional crafts. And one of the most elegant and exquisite crafts is Suzhou fans. Celebrated for their exquisite design and fine quality, they’re popularly associated with literati. How come Suzhou gave birth to such a variety of fans? How can we appreciate their beauty in the modern world? In this episode, we are discovering more stories behind Suzhou fans.

programs.title: Pingyao Hand-Polished Lacquer Art
programs.desc: Pingyao is an old town with a history of over 2,700 years in the north of China, in Shanxi Province. The rich cultural heritage it built up over the centuries eventually gave rise to a very special art—Tuiguang lacquerware. This hand-polished lacquerware, unique to Pingyao, is recognized as one of the four representative lacquerwares of China.

programs.title: CULTURAL INHERITORS When old craftsmanship meets contemporary art
programs.desc: The Vibe production team's meticulously crafted documentary series \"Cultural Inheritors\". The eight episodes span across various regions of China, focusing on the inheritors and artists of intangible cultural heritage. They explore and innovate with artisanal dedication, showcasing the extraordinary craftsmanship of fields such as the Ru Kiln porcelain, colored stone inlay, Pu'er tea, and Lingnan woodwork. Let’s embark on a cultural journey together, savoring the beauty of tradition and embracing the spirit of intangible cultural heritage.

programs.title: Dance of Heroes
programs.desc: In this episode, our host “Uncle Hanzi” travels to Chaonan in Shantou to explore the story of a family devoted to the tradition of Yingge dance. Centered around inheritor Hong Shaohua, the story unfolds through his father’s dedication, his daughter’s passion, and his apprentices’ enthusiasm—showcasing how this centuries-old folk art continues to thrive in the modern world.
